summary: Frontend-only visual regression on HoS → Training → Split Sleeper Trainer → Learn. Verified (1) the new [data-testid=eld-totals] strip is rendered below every EldGrid with per-status chips (OFF/SB/D/OD) and a grand total, and (2) the shift markers were migrated from <polygon> pennants to uniform rounded-rect SVG badges.

verification_results.sl1_arithmetic_note: The segments listed in the review request (OD 00-01=1h, D 01-06=5h, SB 06-13=7h, D 13-17=4h, OFF 17-20=3h, OD 20-24=4h) actually sum to OFF=3, SB=7, D=9, OD=5. The app renders these correctly. The 'expected' OFF=4/OD=4 in the request appears to be an arithmetic slip in the PRD text, NOT an app bug.

action_items: (OPTIONAL) Reconcile the SL1 totals text in the PRD/review description (OFF=4/OD=4 is arithmetically impossible given the listed segments; the app is correctly showing OFF=3/OD=5).
critical_code_review_comments: EldGrid.js: polygon shift marker removed; replaced with a single <rect rx=2> for START/END/CONTINUES — shape is uniform, only width and fill differ. Confirmed no <polygon> inside any [data-testid^=shift-marker-] element.
Totals strip uses `flex flex-wrap` + justify-between — survives narrow viewports without requiring horizontal scroll, solving the originally-reported hidden-totals-column issue.
Clamping of badgeX inside the grid (LABEL_W .. LABEL_W+gridW-flagW) prevents edge overflow for 00:00 and 24:00 badges — verified visually on SL5 CONTINUES badge near 24:00.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Split Sleeper Trainer has 6 example grids on the Learn tab. All render the eld-totals strip + rectangular shift badges correctly. Nav path: Login → HoS tile → Training pill → Split Sleeper Trainer card → Learn tab (default).
